subject: Social and Preventive Medicine
stem: The Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) releases the National Air Quality Index (AQI) for Indian cities. A city records the following sub-index values on a given day: PM2.5 = 185, PM10 = 142, NO2 = 78, SO2 = 60. What is the AQI for that city on that day?
explanation: India's National AQI (CPCB) is calculated as the HIGHEST sub-index among all measured pollutants, not an average. In this scenario, PM2.5 sub-index of 185 is the highest; therefore the city AQI = 185, placing it in the 'Moderate' category (101-200, yellow). This single highest-pollutant-wins rule ensures the worst hazard governs the reported index.
highYield: AQI = highest sub-index among all pollutants tested; PM2.5 sub-index 185 falls in Moderate category (101-200).
pyqNote: SPM fringe — confirmed NEET PG miss

subject: Social and Preventive Medicine
stem: According to India's National Air Quality Index (CPCB), which color band corresponds to the AQI category 'Very Poor'?
explanation: The CPCB National AQI color coding is: Good (0-50, green), Satisfactory (51-100, light green), Moderate (101-200, yellow), Poor (201-300, orange), Very Poor (301-400, red), and Severe (401-500, dark red/maroon). Very Poor corresponds to red, while Severe corresponds to dark red/maroon — a commonly confused pair in MCQs.
highYield: Very Poor AQI = 301-400 = Red; Severe AQI = 401-500 = Dark red/maroon.
pyqNote: SPM fringe — confirmed NEET PG miss

subject: Social and Preventive Medicine
stem: A public health officer notes that the AQI of an industrial city reads 260. Which of the following statements correctly describes this AQI category and the associated health advisory?
options: Moderate (101-200); breathing discomfort to sensitive people
Poor (201-300); breathing discomfort to most people on prolonged exposure
Very Poor (301-400); respiratory illness on prolonged exposure
Severe (401-500); affects healthy people; serious impact on sensitive people
explanation: An AQI of 260 falls in the 'Poor' category (201-300, orange band). In this category, breathing discomfort occurs in most people on prolonged exposure. Very Poor (301-400) leads to respiratory illness on prolonged exposure, and Severe (401-500) affects even healthy individuals. The eight pollutants measured in India's National AQI include PM2.5, PM10, NO2, SO2, O3, CO, NH3, and Pb.
highYield: AQI 201-300 = Poor (orange); breathing discomfort to most people on prolonged exposure.
pyqNote: SPM fringe — confirmed NEET PG miss

subject: Social and Preventive Medicine
stem: As per the Directorate General Factory Advice Service & Labour Institutes (DGFASLI) of India, what is the permissible noise exposure limit for an 8-hour working day?
explanation: Both DGFASLI (India) and OSHA (USA) set the permissible noise exposure limit at 90 dB(A) for an 8-hour working day. Noise-induced hearing loss (NIHL) is the most common occupational disease worldwide. The exchange rate used is 5 dB(A) doubling: for every 5 dB increase, the permissible exposure time halves (e.g., 95 dB(A) = 4 hours, 100 dB(A) = 2 hours).
highYield: Permissible occupational noise = 90 dB(A) for 8 hours (DGFASLI India / OSHA).
pyqNote: SPM fringe — confirmed NEET PG miss

subject: Social and Preventive Medicine
stem: A factory safety officer is assessing workplace lighting. He measures the illuminance in the general office area as 350 lux. Which of the following statements is CORRECT regarding this measurement?
options: 350 lux is inadequate; office work requires at least 500 lux
350 lux is within the recommended range of 200-500 lux for office environments
The SI unit for illuminance is candela, not lux
350 lux exceeds safe limits and may cause glare injury
explanation: The SI unit of illuminance is LUX (lm/m²). Recommended illuminance for office work is 200-500 lux; for passageways and corridors, 50 lux is sufficient. A reading of 350 lux is therefore within the acceptable range for office environments. Candela is the SI unit of luminous intensity, not illuminance — a common distractor in NEET PG questions.
highYield: Illuminance unit = LUX; office work 200-500 lux; passageways 50 lux.
pyqNote: SPM fringe — confirmed NEET PG miss

subject: Social and Preventive Medicine
stem: A chemical plant worker is exposed to a solvent for 15 minutes at a concentration exceeding TLV-TWA but within a defined short-term limit. Which threshold limit value concept applies specifically to a 15-minute maximum exposure?
options: IDLH (Immediately Dangerous to Life or Health)
explanation: TLV-STEL (Short Term Exposure Limit) is the maximum concentration to which workers can be exposed continuously for up to 15 minutes without suffering irritation, chronic tissue damage, or narcosis. TLV-TWA is the 8-hour time-weighted average. TLV-C (Ceiling) must not be exceeded even instantaneously. IDLH is the concentration immediately dangerous to life or health, used for selecting respiratory protection. All TLVs are set by ACGIH (American Conference of Governmental Industrial Hygienists).
highYield: TLV-STEL = 15-minute maximum short-term exposure limit; set by ACGIH.
pyqNote: SPM fringe — confirmed NEET PG miss

subject: Social and Preventive Medicine
stem: Several office employees in a centrally air-conditioned building report headaches, eye irritation, and fatigue during working hours. All symptoms resolve completely on leaving the building. Indoor CO2 monitoring shows levels of 1,100 ppm. What is the MOST LIKELY diagnosis and the key diagnostic criterion?
options: Multiple Chemical Sensitivity; criterion is >500 ppm CO2
Legionnaire's disease; criterion is positive urinary antigen test
Sick Building Syndrome; criterion is symptom resolution on leaving + CO2 >1000 ppm
Building-Related Illness; criterion is symptoms persisting after leaving the building
explanation: Sick Building Syndrome (SBS) is diagnosed when occupants experience non-specific symptoms (headache, eye/nose/throat irritation, fatigue, dizziness) that resolve upon leaving the building. A CO2 level >1000 ppm indicates inadequate ventilation and is a key SBS marker. In contrast, Building-Related Illness (BRI) — such as Legionnaire's disease or Humidifier fever — has identifiable causative agents and symptoms that persist after leaving the building.
highYield: SBS criteria: symptoms resolve on leaving + indoor CO2 >1000 ppm indicating poor ventilation.
pyqNote: SPM fringe — confirmed NEET PG miss

subject: Social and Preventive Medicine
stem: Under India's National STI/RTI Control Programme, a 25-year-old male presents with urethral discharge. Which color-coded syndromic management kit should be dispensed?
explanation: The Red kit under India's National STI/RTI Control Programme is for urethral discharge in males, covering gonorrhoea and chlamydia with syndromic management (tab ciprofloxacin + tab doxycycline). Blue kit = vaginal discharge in females; Green kit = genital ulcer disease; Yellow kit = lower abdominal pain/PID in females.
highYield: Red kit = urethral discharge in males (ciprofloxacin + doxycycline).
pyqNote: SPM fringe — confirmed NEET PG miss

subject: Social and Preventive Medicine
stem: A 30-year-old woman presents to a PHC with vaginal discharge. The ANM follows India's National STI/RTI Control Programme guidelines. Which color kit should be given, and what is the correct drug regimen?
explanation: The Blue kit is designated for vaginal discharge in females under India's syndromic management approach for STI/RTI. The regimen includes metronidazole (for bacterial vaginosis/trichomoniasis) and fluconazole (for candidiasis). Yellow kit is for lower abdominal pain/PID and contains cefixime + doxycycline + metronidazole. Green kit covers genital ulcer disease with benzathine penicillin + ciprofloxacin.
highYield: Blue kit = vaginal discharge in females; drugs = metronidazole + fluconazole.
pyqNote: SPM fringe — confirmed NEET PG miss

subject: Social and Preventive Medicine
stem: A 28-year-old woman presents with lower abdominal pain, fever, and mucopurulent cervical discharge at a Community Health Centre. The medical officer decides to use syndromic management under the National STI/RTI Control Programme. Which kit and regimen is MOST appropriate?
explanation: Lower abdominal pain with cervicitis in a female represents the PID/lower abdominal pain syndrome, managed with the Yellow kit under India's National STI/RTI Control Programme. The regimen (cefixime + doxycycline + metronidazole) covers Neisseria gonorrhoeae, Chlamydia trachomatis, and anaerobes — the typical polymicrobial aetiology of PID. Red kit is for males with urethral discharge; Green kit is for genital ulcer disease.
highYield: Yellow kit = PID/lower abdominal pain in females; cefixime + doxycycline + metronidazole.
pyqNote: SPM fringe — confirmed NEET PG miss

subject: Social and Preventive Medicine
stem: Which international agency is responsible for the International Health Regulations (IHR 2005) and is headquartered in Geneva?
explanation: The World Health Organization (WHO), headquartered in Geneva, Switzerland, is responsible for the International Health Regulations (IHR 2005) — the legal framework requiring countries to report public health events of international concern (PHEIC). UNICEF and UNFPA are headquartered in New York; the World Bank is in Washington DC. WHO was established on 7 April 1948 (now celebrated as World Health Day).
highYield: WHO = Geneva; custodian of IHR 2005 and declaration of PHEIC.
pyqNote: SPM fringe — confirmed NEET PG miss

subject: Social and Preventive Medicine
stem: The Codex Alimentarius Commission, which sets international food safety standards, is a joint body of WHO and which other UN agency headquartered in Rome?
explanation: The Codex Alimentarius Commission was established in 1963 as a joint FAO/WHO body to develop international food standards, guidelines, and codes of practice. FAO (Food and Agriculture Organization) is headquartered in Rome, Italy. The Codex standards protect consumer health and facilitate fair international trade in food. UNICEF (child health, New York) and UNFPA (reproductive health, New York) are different agencies.
highYield: Codex Alimentarius = joint FAO/WHO body; FAO headquartered in Rome.
pyqNote: SPM fringe — confirmed NEET PG miss

subject: Social and Preventive Medicine
stem: The Colombo Plan, established in 1951, primarily focuses on which of the following?
options: Regulation of pharmaceutical patent rights in Asia
Technical cooperation for socioeconomic and health development in Asia-Pacific
International disease surveillance and outbreak response
Maternal and child nutrition supplementation in South Asia
explanation: The Colombo Plan (1951) is an intergovernmental regional organization established to promote technical cooperation for socioeconomic development, including health, in the Asia-Pacific region. It was inaugurated at the Commonwealth Conference in Colombo, Sri Lanka. It is distinct from WHO (disease surveillance), UNICEF (child nutrition), and TRIPS/WTO (pharmaceutical patents). Membership now includes 27 countries spanning Asia-Pacific.
highYield: Colombo Plan 1951 = technical cooperation for Asia-Pacific socioeconomic and health development.
pyqNote: SPM fringe — confirmed NEET PG miss

subject: Social and Preventive Medicine
stem: What is the minimum free residual chlorine required at the consumer's tap in a water distribution system, as per standard water quality guidelines?
explanation: A minimum free residual chlorine of 0.2 mg/L must be maintained at the consumer's tap (farthest point in the distribution system) to ensure microbiological safety of drinking water. At the treatment plant, a higher residual is maintained to allow for decay in the distribution network. Residual below 0.2 mg/L at the tap indicates inadequate chlorination or re-contamination in pipes.
highYield: Minimum free residual chlorine at consumer's tap = 0.2 mg/L.
pyqNote: SPM fringe — confirmed NEET PG miss

subject: Social and Preventive Medicine
stem: An infant in a rural village develops cyanosis and methaemoglobinaemia after feeding on formula prepared with well water. Testing reveals a nitrate level of 60 mg/L. Which of the following statements is CORRECT?
options: Nitrate level is within WHO safe limits of <100 mg/L
Nitrate level exceeds the WHO guideline of <45 mg/L and is the likely cause of blue baby syndrome
Methaemoglobinaemia at this nitrate level affects adults more than infants
The safe nitrate level for drinking water is <10 mg/L as per WHO
explanation: The WHO guideline for nitrate in drinking water is <45 mg/L (as nitrate). Levels >45 mg/L cause blue baby syndrome (infantile methaemoglobinaemia) because gut bacteria in infants convert nitrate to nitrite, which oxidises haemoglobin to methaemoglobin. Adults are less susceptible due to higher gastric acidity inhibiting gut bacterial conversion and higher methaemoglobin reductase activity. A level of 60 mg/L clearly exceeds the safe limit.
highYield: Nitrate safe limit = <45 mg/L (WHO); >45 mg/L causes blue baby syndrome in infants.
pyqNote: SPM fringe — confirmed NEET PG miss

subject: Social and Preventive Medicine
stem: A community in an endemic fluorosis zone uses drinking water with a fluoride level of 2.5 mg/L. What is the MOST LIKELY fluorosis manifestation at this concentration?
options: No effect; fluoride up to 3 mg/L is safe
explanation: Fluoride levels: 0.5-0.8 mg/L is the optimal/acceptable range; >1.5 mg/L causes dental fluorosis (mottled enamel); >3-6 mg/L causes skeletal fluorosis (osteosclerosis, crippling). At 2.5 mg/L, dental fluorosis is the expected and most common manifestation. Skeletal fluorosis typically requires prolonged exposure to >3 mg/L. Thyroid disruption is associated with excess iodine or goitrogens, not fluoride at this level.
highYield: Fluoride >1.5 mg/L = dental fluorosis; >3-6 mg/L = skeletal fluorosis.
pyqNote: SPM fringe — confirmed NEET PG miss

subject: Social and Preventive Medicine
stem: In bacteriological examination of drinking water, what is the acceptable MPN (Most Probable Number) of coliform organisms per 100 mL?
explanation: The MPN of coliform organisms in drinking water must be ZERO per 100 mL to be considered microbiologically safe. Any detection of coliforms (total or faecal) indicates faecal contamination and is unacceptable. The WHO also recommends 0 E. coli per 100 mL in treated piped water. WHO acceptable turbidity is <1 NTU (preferred) and <4 NTU (not objectionable) — a separate but related standard.
highYield: Acceptable MPN of coliforms in drinking water = 0 per 100 mL.
pyqNote: SPM fringe — confirmed NEET PG miss

subject: Social and Preventive Medicine
stem: A 22-year-old male labourer from Bihar presents with progressive spastic paraplegia of the lower limbs. He has no sensory loss, no bladder involvement, and reports consuming a local grain-based diet for months during drought. Which of the following is the MOST LIKELY causative toxin?
explanation: Lathyrism is caused by BOAA (beta-oxalyl-amino-alanine), a neurotoxic amino acid found in the grass pea Lathyrus sativus (khesari dal). It primarily affects young adult males, causing irreversible spastic paraplegia without sensory loss or sphincter dysfunction. It is prevalent during droughts when khesari dal is consumed in excess as a food substitute. Sanguinarine causes epidemic dropsy; aflatoxin B1 causes hepatocellular carcinoma; ergotamine causes gangrenous/convulsive ergotism.
highYield: Lathyrism = BOAA from Lathyrus sativus; spastic paraplegia in young adult males.
pyqNote: SPM fringe — confirmed NEET PG miss

subject: Social and Preventive Medicine
stem: During a festival, several people in a village develop bilateral pitting oedema of the legs, erythema, and diarrhoea after consuming adulterated mustard oil. Skin biopsy shows capillary dilatation. Which toxin and its source is responsible?
options: Sanguinarine from Argemone mexicana oil contaminating mustard oil
explanation: Epidemic dropsy is caused by contamination of mustard oil with Argemone mexicana (Mexican prickly poppy) oil, which contains the toxic glucoside sanguinarine. Sanguinarine causes capillary leak syndrome leading to oedema, erythema of skin (especially legs), diarrhoea, and in severe cases, glaucoma and cardiac failure. Outbreaks have occurred repeatedly in Delhi and other Indian states. The disease is NOT caused by ergot, lathyrus, or aflatoxin.
highYield: Epidemic dropsy = argemone oil (sanguinarine) contaminates mustard oil; capillary leak syndrome.
pyqNote: SPM fringe — confirmed NEET PG miss

subject: Social and Preventive Medicine
stem: Which of the following statements about aflatoxin is CORRECT?
options: Aflatoxin is produced by Claviceps purpurea on wheat
Aflatoxin B1 is the most potent and is associated with hepatocellular carcinoma; it resists autoclaving
Aflatoxin is destroyed by boiling at 100°C for 30 minutes
Aflatoxin G1 is more potent than B1
explanation: Aflatoxin is produced by Aspergillus flavus and Aspergillus parasiticus, primarily contaminating groundnuts, maize, and grains. Aflatoxin B1 is the most potent naturally occurring hepatocarcinogen (IARC Group 1). A critical property: aflatoxins are HEAT STABLE and resist autoclaving (121°C, 15 psi), making processing ineffective once contamination occurs. Aflatoxin B1 > B2 > G1 > G2 in order of potency. Claviceps purpurea produces ergot alkaloids, not aflatoxin.
highYield: Aflatoxin B1 = most potent; from Aspergillus flavus on groundnuts; heat stable — resists autoclaving.
pyqNote: SPM fringe — confirmed NEET PG miss

subject: Social and Preventive Medicine
stem: An outbreak occurs in a community where rye bread was the staple food. Affected individuals develop intense burning sensation of the extremities followed by dry gangrene. Some others develop convulsions. The causative organism is:
explanation: Ergotism is caused by Claviceps purpurea, a fungus that parasitises rye, wheat, and other grains. It produces ergot alkaloids with powerful vasoconstrictive and neurotoxic properties. There are two clinical forms: (1) Gangrenous ergotism — vasospasm causing burning sensations ('St Anthony's Fire') followed by dry gangrene of extremities; (2) Convulsive ergotism — CNS effects with epileptiform seizures. Aspergillus flavus causes aflatoxicosis; Fusarium causes fumonisins; Penicillium citrinum produces citrinin.
highYield: Ergotism = Claviceps purpurea on rye; two types: gangrenous (vasospasm) and convulsive.
pyqNote: SPM fringe — confirmed NEET PG miss

subject: Social and Preventive Medicine
stem: The WHO STEPwise approach to Surveillance (STEPS) for non-communicable diseases is conducted in three steps. Which step involves BIOCHEMICAL measurements such as blood glucose and cholesterol?
explanation: The WHO STEPS framework for NCD surveillance has three core steps: Step 1 = Questionnaire (self-reported information on tobacco, alcohol, diet, physical activity, history of raised BP/glucose); Step 2 = Physical measurements (height, weight, waist circumference, blood pressure, heart rate); Step 3 = Biochemical measurements (fasting blood glucose, total cholesterol, urinary sodium). This stepwise approach allows countries to build surveillance capacity progressively.
highYield: STEPS: Step 1 = questionnaire; Step 2 = physical measurements; Step 3 = biochemical measurements.
pyqNote: SPM fringe — confirmed NEET PG miss

subject: Social and Preventive Medicine
stem: The National Programme for Prevention and Control of Cancer, Diabetes, CVD and Stroke (NPCDCS) in India primarily operates at which level of the health system for opportunistic screening?
options: Primary Health Centre and above (PHC, CHC, District Hospital)
Only at tertiary care AIIMS centres
explanation: NPCDCS (launched 2010, now merged under National NCD Mission) operates through the primary healthcare system at PHC, CHC, and district hospital levels for opportunistic screening of common NCDs — cancers (oral, breast, cervical), diabetes, hypertension, CVD, and stroke. Health and Wellness Centres (CPHC model) now serve as the frontline for NCD screening, with district NCD clinics for management and cardiac care units at district level.
highYield: NPCDCS operates at PHC and above for opportunistic NCD screening; now part of National NCD Mission.
pyqNote: SPM fringe — confirmed NEET PG miss

subject: Social and Preventive Medicine
stem: NPHCE, a national programme under India's Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, specifically targets which population group?
explanation: NPHCE stands for the National Programme for Health Care of the Elderly, targeting persons aged 60 years and above. It provides geriatric services at all levels: dedicated geriatric OPDs at district hospitals, geriatric wards at regional/state medical colleges, and domiciliary visits at the community level. It was launched in 2010 under the 11th Five-Year Plan to address the growing burden of age-related morbidity in India.
highYield: NPHCE = National Programme for Health Care of the Elderly (60+ years); launched 2010.
pyqNote: SPM fringe — confirmed NEET PG miss

subject: Social and Preventive Medicine
stem: During a fire in a chemical storage facility, rescue workers enter without respiratory protection. The concept used to decide the threshold concentration above which entry requires full self-contained breathing apparatus is:
options: TLV-C — ceiling value not to be exceeded at any time
IDLH — Immediately Dangerous to Life or Health
explanation: IDLH (Immediately Dangerous to Life or Health) is defined by NIOSH as any atmosphere that poses an immediate threat to life, causes irreversible adverse health effects, or impairs an individual's ability to escape. It is the criterion for mandating full self-contained breathing apparatus (SCBA) during emergency rescue. TLV-TWA governs routine 8-hour exposures; TLV-STEL is for 15-minute peaks; TLV-C is for instantaneous ceiling — none of these specifically govern emergency SCBA selection.
highYield: IDLH = immediately dangerous to life/health; threshold for mandatory SCBA in emergency entry.
pyqNote: SPM fringe — confirmed NEET PG miss
